name: Parque Natural de las Dunas de Liencres y playa de La Arnía – circular por los acantilados
region_intro: Mountain biking around Santa Cruz De Bezana offers a diverse landscape where the Cantabrian coast meets varied inland terrain. The region features coastal paths, extensive dune formations, and maritime pine forests, providing a mix of flat and undulating routes. Further inland, the landscape transitions to hills and more rugged areas, offering opportunities for elevation gain and technical sections. This geographical blend ensures a wide range of mountain biking experiences, from scenic coastal rides to more challenging trails through natural parks. Best mountain bike trails around Santa Cruz De Bezana The most popular mountain bike trail is Parque Natural de las Dunas de Liencres y playa de La Arnía – circular por los acantilados, a 14.7 miles (23.6 km) difficult trail that takes 1 hour 48 minutes to complete. This route explores the coastal natural park with views of dunes and cliffs. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Boo de Piélagos train station – Usgo Beach loop from Bezana, a moderate 33.7 miles (54.2 km) path. This route features a mix of coastal and inland scenery, including a beach section. Local mountain bikers also love the Boo de Piélagos train station – Puente de Arce loop from Mompía-Mortera, a 23.3 miles (37.5 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 2 hours 14 minutes. Mountain biking around Santa Cruz De Bezana is defined by coastal paths, dune landscapes, and forested areas, with some routes offering significant elevation changes. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy coastal rides to more challenging inland trails. The routes in Santa Cruz De Bezana are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from more than 25 reviews. More than 350 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Santa Cruz De Bezana's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails around Santa Cruz De Bezana?
A: The terrain around Santa Cruz De Bezana is quite varied, offering a mix of coastal paths, extensive dune formations, and maritime pine forests closer to the sea. Inland, you'll find hills and more rugged areas with opportunities for elevation gain and technical sections. Some routes feature both paved and unpaved surfaces, with challenging segments that might require pushing your bike, especially in the more mountainous areas of Cantabria.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Santa Cruz De Bezana?
A: Yes, Santa Cruz De Bezana offers trails suitable for beginners. Out of the 14 mountain bike routes available, 3 are classified as easy. An example of an easy route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40323702" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the S-10 Highway loop from Bezana&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 30.5 km.

Q: Can I find challenging mountain bike trails for advanced riders?
A: Absolutely. For advanced riders seeking a challenge, there is at least one difficult trail available. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e999797581/parque-natural-de-las-dunas-de-liencres-y-playa-de-la-arnia-circular-por-los-acantilados" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parque Natural de las Dunas de Liencres y playa de La Arnía – circular por los acantilados&lt;/a&gt; is a 23.6 km difficult route that explores the coastal natural park with views of dunes and cliffs, offering a demanding experience.

Q: Are there any family-friendly mountain bike options in the area?
A: While specific 'family-friendly' designations aren't always explicit, the region's 'Vías Verdes' (Greenways), which are converted former railway lines, offer gentler and more accessible options ideal for families and beginners. These routes, such as the Vía Verde del Pas and Vía Verde del Besaya, provide beautiful natural settings away from traffic.

Q: What kind of views or natural attractions can I expect along the mountain bike trails?
A: The trails offer diverse scenery. You can enjoy spectacular dune formations and coastal views in areas like the Liencres Dunes Natural Park. Inland routes provide views of hills and forests. Notable viewpoints include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3691513" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View from Monte Picota&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4073927" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Viewpoint of Alto de la Cigüela&lt;/a&gt;. You might also encounter natural features like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3943225" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pozo Tremeo and Forest Path&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5286774" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Viewpoint of Marisma del Conde&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there mountain bike routes that form a loop?
A: Yes, many mountain bike routes in Santa Cruz De Bezana are designed as loops. Examples include the difficult &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e999797581/parque-natural-de-las-dunas-de-liencres-y-playa-de-la-arnia-circular-por-los-acantilados" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parque Natural de las Dunas de Liencres y playa de La Arnía – circular por los acantilados&lt;/a&gt;, the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38228384" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Boo de Piélagos train station – Usgo Beach loop from Bezana&lt;/a&gt;, and the easy &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40323702" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the S-10 Highway loop from Bezana&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Santa Cruz De Bezana?
A: The mountain bike trails around Santa Cruz De Bezana are highly regarded by the komoot community, holding an average rating of 4.5 stars from over 25 reviews. Many riders appreciate the diverse landscape, which combines scenic coastal rides with more challenging inland trails through natural parks.

Q: Are there any specific peaks or mountains I can explore by mountain bike?
A: While the broader Cantabrian Mountains offer extensive terrain, within the immediate vicinity, you can find routes that incorporate features like Monte Picota. For example, the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40371467" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View from Monte Picota – Canallave Beach loop from Soto de la Marina&lt;/a&gt; includes views from Monte Picota. Other notable summits in the area include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/810123" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñas Blancas Summit&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3927126" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monte Tolío&lt;/a&gt;, which offer panoramic views.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Santa Cruz De Bezana?
A: Santa Cruz De Bezana benefits from a coastal climate. While mountain biking is possible year-round, spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant conditions with milder temperatures and less extreme weather, making them ideal for exploring both coastal and inland routes. Summer can be warm, especially inland, while winter might bring cooler temperatures and occasional rain, though many trails remain accessible.

Q: Are there any attractions or parks nearby that can be combined with a mountain bike trip?
A: Yes, the region offers several attractions. The Liencres Dunes Natural Park and Costa Quebrada are directly adjacent, providing spectacular natural beauty. Further afield, the Cabárceno Nature Park, a re-naturalized former open-pit mine, offers 20 kilometers of paths to explore its unique karst landscape and observe wildlife, accessible by bike, foot, or car. You can also visit urban parks like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4492468" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Llamas Atlantic Park&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/586772" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Vaguada de Las Llamas Park&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: How many mountain bike trails are available in Santa Cruz De Bezana?
A: There are 14 dedicated mountain bike trails around Santa Cruz De Bezana, catering to various skill levels from easy to difficult.
tips: Very popular beach and visited by the whole family, a magnificent place at any time of the year. The cliff path allows for a very scenic drive with beautiful views at every turn.

It is located in the vicinity of the point of the same name and is divided into two parts: the eastern, very small and rocky, and the western, somewhat larger and sandy.

Its name comes from the caves between the rocks at the tip of Somocuevas. Nearby there is an islet that is frequented by seagulls and cormorants.

It has a length of 100 meters and its access is through a staircase of 139 steps that descends from a high cliff located to the west of the beach. This staircase is reached by a narrow path that comes from the parking area located a few hundred meters from the stairs. The slope of access to the water is gentle and the waves are also gentle because it is protected from the open sea by an inlet. The bottom is partly rocky although fine sand is also abundant. It is frequented by nudists, it is not official nudist as such. It is freely accessible.

The surroundings are made up of Atlantic meadows and residential developments. To the west is the natural park of the Dunas de Liencres, one of the most important coastal points of the Cantabrian Sea.

Arnía beach is located in the natural park of the Dunas de Liencres and Costa Quebrada, between the municipalities of Piélagos and Santa Cruz de Bezana, in the autonomous community of Cantabria, Spain. The beach is located in a rocky environment, with rocks that are more than 90 million years old.
It is worth a careful visit to the place.
